comment: Cosmologically-coupled-black-hole (CCBH) coupling index k. Croker & Weiner (2019, ApJ 882 19) show a cosmologically coupled compact object gains mass M(a) ~ a^k, with k = -3 w_in fixed by the interior equation of state w_in; the population energy density then scales rho ~ a^(k-3), i.e. w_eff = -k/3. A de Sitter (vacuum, w_in=-1) interior gives k=3 EXACTLY, hence w_eff=-1 (a cosmological constant). Farrah+2023 (ApJL 944 L31) measured k from the redshift growth of SMBH mass at fixed stellar mass in passively-evolving ellipticals. The CCBH-as-dark-energy interpretation is CONTESTED (see contestation[]).

tfpt_prediction.note: TFPT 'black hole local, de Sitter global': the Nariai seam interior is the de Sitter vacuum (w_in=-1, S_dS*rho_Lambda=32 pi^4), so k = -3 w_in = 3 exactly and the CCBH population acts as a true cosmological constant (w=-1). Same w=-1 confronted with DESI in dark-energy-w-watchdog.

density_closure.source: Farrah+2023 (SMBH realization density grown via k=3) vs Planck 2018 Omega_Lambda
density_closure.note: model-dependent and contested; the CCBH-side prediction (~0.68) carries a large model uncertainty (~0.1) that dominates -> reported as an order-level consistency leg, NOT a sharp pull

contestation: Lacy et al. 2024 (no required mass growth in some samples)
Amendola et al. 2023 (background-cosmology constraints disfavour k=3 as all of DE)
Mistele 2023 (gauge/averaging concerns in the coupling derivation)
